draft.introduction: Robert Pirsig’s Zen and the Art of Motorcycle Maintenance (1974) is a philosophical novel exploring the concept of Quality as the fundamental reality underlying both classical and romantic modes of understanding. Reference entry only; full text under copyright.

translation.original_ref: Pirsig, Zen and the Art of Motorcycle Maintenance (1974)
translation.text: [This entry serves as a reference placeholder. The full text of Zen and the Art of Motorcycle Maintenance remains under copyright. The work explores the metaphysics of Quality through the narrative of a motorcycle journey across the American West.]

translator_notes.note: Pirsig’s “Metaphysics of Quality” argues that Quality is not a subjective judgment but the primary empirical reality from which both subjects and objects are derived. This places him in conversation with process philosophy (Whitehead) and Zen Buddhism’s emphasis on direct experience prior to conceptual division.
